explicit_text: I first became aware of the attacks on the World Trade Center shortly after the planes struck, while I was in my office on the 7th floor of the Main Interior building. Someone came down the hallway excitedly telling me about the planes hitting the towers, and I immediately began coordinating emergency responses. The World Trade Center was clearly a major target, and its attack set off a chain of urgent security measures across Washington, D.C. and beyond.

explicit_text: Shortly after the World Trade Center was hit, I heard reports that a plane had struck the Pentagon. I knew we had to respond quickly, so I dispatched U.S. Park Police officers there, including motorcycle officers who arrived soon after the impact. The Pentagon was a critical military target, and we coordinated evacuations and security around it, including helicopter evacuations of injured and deceased personnel.

explicit_text: On 9/11, Washington, D.C. became the center of intense security activity as we tried to assess and respond to potential threats. I drove around the city inspecting monuments like the Jefferson and Lincoln Memorials and positioned myself on Memorial Bridge due to a suspicious package. Roadblocks were set up, and we coordinated evacuations and security measures across the downtown core to protect key sites and personnel.

explicit_text: As a major with the U.S. Park Police, I took immediate action on 9/11 to ensure the security of national monuments and memorials. We quickly mobilized officers, called in personnel on days off, and secured sites like the Jefferson and Lincoln Memorials. The U.S. Park Police also assisted with evacuations at the Pentagon and helped establish command centers to coordinate our response efforts.

explicit_text: Memorial Bridge was a key location on 9/11 because I discovered a suspicious package on the sidewalk there. I immediately called it in and had both ends of the bridge shut down until a canine unit could inspect the box. This action took about 30 to 45 minutes, during which we diverted traffic and secured the area to prevent any further threat.

explicit_text: I was working in the Department of the Interior on 9/11 when the attacks occurred.
